description:
Create a GitLab CI pipeline with two stages, Test and Release, using python images. In the Test stage add a "Run tox" job that uses the python:3.6 Docker image, runs a before_script command `pip install -e .[tests]` and then executes `tox`. In the Release stage add a "Patch release" job that uses the default python image, runs a before_script sequence: `eval $(ssh-agent -s)`, `mkdir -p ~/.ssh`, `ssh-add <(echo "$SSH_PRIVATE_KEY")`, `echo -e "Host *
	StrictHostKeyChecking no

" > ~/.ssh/config`, `pip install -U bumpversion twine`, `git config --global user.email $GITLAB_USER_EMAIL`, `git config --global user.name "Gitlab CI"`, and `git checkout master`; then runs `bumpversion patch ./VERSION --commit --tag --message 'Releasing {new_version}'`, `git push --tags git@gitlab.com:$CI_PROJECT_PATH.git master`, `python setup.py sdist`, and `twine upload dist/*`. Configure the job to run only on the master branch of the hadrien/aws_lambda_logging project and require manual triggering, and set the variable GIT_STRATEGY to clone.
